expectations.evidence: lib/turbosign.ts exists as a TurboSign config/client file

lib/turbosign.ts calls TurboSign.configure({ apiKey: process.env.TURBODOCX_API_KEY, orgId: process.env.TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, senderEmail: process.env.TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL, senderName: process.env.TURBODOCX_SENDER_NAME, baseUrl: process.env.TURBODOCX_BASE_URL })
API routes created at app/api/turbosign/send/route.ts and app/api/turbosign/status/[documentId]/route.ts in the app/api/ directory
app/api/turbosign/send/route.ts exports 'export async function POST(request: NextRequest)'; status route exports 'export async function GET('
No main app file wires routes — file-based routing is used via app/api/ directory structure
.env.local contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL; .env.example also has both
No file contains TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY anywhere in the project
Generated files use .ts and .tsx extensions (route.ts, turbosign.ts, page.tsx)
Both route handlers import and use NextRequest/NextResponse from 'next/server'
